domain_score_reviews.reasoning: REPowerEU gas regulation (EU/2026/261) entered force 2 February 2026 with legally binding phased ban on Russian LNG/pipeline gas to 2027, including third-country authorization and diversification mandates. This permanent regulation structurally ends EU-Russia energy interdependence, meeting upgrade threshold via confirmed institutional implementation.

Russian submarine reconnaissance of undersea cables/pipelines continues pattern since Nord Stream 2022, Balticconnector 2023, Estlink 2024. UK/Norway military response confirms peacetime survey/sabotage potential per Defence Secretary statement.

GRU-linked political technologists dispatched to Hungary elections under Kiriyenko mirroring Moldova 2024 playbook.

Anti-circumvention tool creates €650B fiscal space for defence; REPowerEU permanently restructures EU-Russia energy trade.

analyst_briefing: Recommend defence/energy/trade upgrades (3→4) based on structural EDF/PESCO fiscal integration, REPowerEU permanent Russian gas ban, and anti-circumvention sanctions tool—each confirmed Tier 1 with multi-source institutional implementation. Tech/diplomatic hold amid AI manipulation vulnerabilities offset by US NDS deprioritization vs robust NATO-EU cooperation. Russian hybrid tempo accelerates across FIMI (Euromore/Pravfond sanctions), elections (Hungary GRU), grey-zone (submarine recon)—all continuations requiring no registry additions. US doctrine evolution structurally shifts burden to EU capability. Escalate wdm (Hungary interference HIGH), scem/gmm (sanctions/infra HIGH), fcw/agm (FIMI/AI MEDIUM). No contested findings; all source-verified primary institutional reporting.
